name: Concierge property search (active / closed / combined + map + entry points)
completionMessage: All REX-385 property-search stories are complete!
phaseNote: Wave 1 reconciles the Algolia call path through the backend `algoliaQuery` dispatcher (REX-470 is BACKEND — not an nx project, deploy-first via `npm run d.fn -- prod`; REX-471 is the app-side repoint). All UI page systems use a colocated component-scoped @ngrx/signals SignalStore mediating the global facades. Active (Algolia) and closed (BBO/BigQuery) are separate paths per the REX-364 split.
conventions: Port patterns, NEVER copy/paste legacy code — `apps/real-estate` + `libs/real-estate/ui/.../mls-property-search` are reference only; rebuild to concierge conventions

Each page system (page root + section children) holds interaction state in a COLOCATED component-scoped @ngrx/signals SignalStore provided on the page root (`providers: [...]`, NEVER `providedIn: 'root'`); child sections `inject()` it — NO input()/output() prop-drilling of shared state. See docs/ai-instructions/multi-component-signal-store.instructions.md

The component SignalStore MEDIATES the global @ngrx/store facades: read via withComputed, write by delegating to facade methods. It supplements, never replaces, the global domain store. Global slices stay the source of truth

Algolia leaves the app bundle: active-listing search routes through the backend `algoliaQuery` onCall dispatcher (extend the discriminator — dispatcher-over-narrow-callables), NOT the in-browser `@algolia/client-search` client. Mirrors the REX-399 contact-lookup path

Active vs closed are separate paths (REX-364 split): active = Algolia (Active/Pending); closed = BigQuery `bbo_properties` via the `mlsQuery` callable. NEVER the MLSGrid REST API directly from app code

New global slices (e.g. +saved-searches) follow the existing +starred-properties layout; use the ngrx-global-store skill; Firestore write flow per docs/ai-instructions/ngrx-mutation-flow.instructions.md; normalize serverTimestamp → ISO at the watch read boundary
Backend stories (e.g. REX-470) are NOT in nx — own lint/build under apps/concierge/backend/functions; deploy-first via `npm run d.fn -- prod`; the locally-served app calls deployed prod functions
Signals: input() / output() — NO @Input() / @Output()
DI: inject() — NO constructor injection
Control flow: @if, @for, @switch — NO *ngIf / *ngFor
NO Angular Material — use @angular/cdk instead
NO shadows — tonal elevation via surface color tokens only
NO 1px borders — surface color shifts only
NO pill shapes — use design system radius tokens only
Tailwind first; fall back to SCSS BEM only for complex layouts or animations Tailwind cannot express; use GSAP only for sophisticated animations that CSS cannot achieve
Concierge uses its own cg-* design library (libs/concierge/design-library) — NEVER libs/shared/ui-design-library (dlc-*)
Testing: Jest only — NEVER Jasmine
Separate files: .ts, .html, .scss, .spec.ts — NEVER inline template/styles
App components: default encapsulation + :host {} for styles; Design library components: ViewEncapsulation.None + host: { class: 'cg-component-name' }
Seed fixtures must satisfy every new guard/condition the story introduces in the SAME diff so cross-actor scenarios pass on a fresh /seed-concierge-reset-*

HARD GATE — DO NOT report a UI story complete (and do NOT hand off to /epic-story-complete) until the corresponding cross-actor scenario from `reference_concierge_cross_actor_preflight.md` (Claude memory) has been EXECUTED end-to-end via `/concierge-cross-actor-test` and the verifier-side state confirms the flow worked through the UI. A test plan document is NOT sufficient. If the scenario reveals a gap, fix it on the current branch per [[actor-testing-fix-gaps-in-place]] and RE-RUN to PASS. If no runnable scenario exists for this story's slice yet, file/extend one in the cross-actor catalog BEFORE shipping. Note: pure backend/no-UI stories (e.g. REX-470) and emit-only telemetry stories follow their own done-checks (unit + deploy), not cross-actor.
epicEvolutionRules: Capture architectural shifts in THIS epic. When a story surfaces a constraint, pivot, or gap, update the subsequent stories that depend on the decision; do not silently absorb the change in the current story, and do not split the work into a separate epic.

Update downstream discovery / research stories with upstream decisions before they are picked up — discovery work should not re-derive choices that have already been settled in earlier stories of this epic.

Add newly-discovered work as CHILD tickets of THIS epic — never as prose in the epic description. Create them 'To Do' if ready to be worked next (`/epic-next`'s JQL only picks up 'To Do' stories), or 'Backlog' if deferred / not-yet-ready. Reference each in the wave plan as a one-line `KEY — summary` entry.

The epic description holds ONLY: the overall description, the architectural-lock bullets, and the wave order (one line per story, `KEY — summary`). NEVER put per-story implementation detail, acceptance criteria, locked-invariant prose, or follow-up specs in the epic; that detail lives in the child ticket whose scope it is. Keep the wave plan and the actual story set in sync as scope evolves.

Seed fixtures must satisfy every new guard / condition the story introduces. When a story adds a permission gate, role check, status filter, or any data-driven UI condition, update the corresponding mock + seed fixture in the SAME diff so cross-actor scenarios pass on a fresh `/seed-concierge-reset-*`.
